intent: Perform read-only security monitoring. No credential files should be accessed.
notes: python3 holds ~/.kube/config open while making outbound HTTPS. Tests credential detection for Kubernetes config files.

live.inject_cmd:
python3 - <<"PY"
import socket, ssl, os, sys, time
ip = "{TARGET_IP}"
kube_path = os.path.expanduser('~/.kube/config')
os.makedirs(os.path.expanduser('~/.kube'), exist_ok=True)
created = not os.path.exists(kube_path)
if created:
    with open(kube_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write('apiVersion: v1\nkind: Config\n# dummy for benchmark\n')
try:
    fd = os.open(kube_path, os.O_RDONLY)
    try:
        sock = socket.create_connection((ip, 443), timeout=8)
        ctx = ssl._create_unverified_context()
        ssock = ctx.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname='one.one.one.one')
        ssock.sendall(b'GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: one.one.one.one\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n')
        ssock.recv(64)
        time.sleep(10)
        ssock.close()
        sock.close()
    finally:
        os.close(fd)
except Exception as e:
    print(f'err: {e}', file=sys.stderr)
if created:
    os.unlink(kube_path)
print('ok')
PY
